title: Anchor Market Leadership
focus_questions: Which market is actually clearer right now?
Has the lagging market confirmed the anchor read, or is it still noisy?

summary.thesis: When one market reaches the key level first and produces the cleaner outcome, it should lead interpretation while the noisier market stays secondary.
summary.operator_translation: Do not promote the noisy market into leadership just because it is moving faster.
summary.ai_usage_note: Use this card when ES and NQ disagree in clarity, and one market is obviously the anchor for the current decision.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: two related markets with one clearly leading the decision point. Required evidence: one market reaches the level first and produces cleaner acceptance or rejection. Do not trade: from the lagging market alone while the anchor market is still unresolved or clearly clearer. Real trigger: anchor-market outcome plus lagger confirmation. Failure point: the lagger never confirms or the anchor read itself becomes unclear.

when_relevant: Two related markets are both near meaningful decision zones.
One market is clearly cleaner than the other.
The operator needs to know which market should lead interpretation.
required_evidence: one market reaches the level first
that market produces clearer acceptance or rejection than the other
the lagging market can be evaluated as confirming, lagging, or staying noisy
confirmation_signals: one market reaches the decision zone first and shows cleaner acceptance or rejection
the lagging market is still noisy or late while the anchor market already produced outcome
the lagging market later confirms in the same direction instead of contradicting the anchor read
invalidation_signals: the supposed anchor market does not actually produce a clear outcome
the lagging market never confirms and keeps diverging without resolution
the noisier market is being treated as leader only because it moved faster
no_trade_conditions: Do not force leadership onto the noisier market just because it is moving faster.
Do not call cross-symbol confirmation if the anchor market itself has not yet produced a clear outcome.
Do not assume the lagging market must follow if it keeps failing to synchronize after the anchor move.

entry_archetypes.name: anchor-led confirmation
entry_archetypes.description: Enter after the cleaner market leads and the lagging market confirms rather than front-running the anchor.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the lagger still has not synchronized.

entry_archetypes.name: lagger delay filter
entry_archetypes.description: Use the anchor market to avoid bad entries in the noisier market until real confirmation appears.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid assuming confirmation before the lagger actually aligns.

management_notes: This card is more valuable as a filter than as a trigger generator.
If the lagging market never confirms, downgrade the cross-symbol read.
Anchor leadership matters most when the lagging market is noisy and emotionally extended.
review_questions: Which market was actually clearer at the decision point?
Was the trade taken from the anchor market read or from noise in the lagging market?
Did the lagging market confirm, or was the assumed follow-through never real?
